_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-3 Newborn/Neonatal Assessment and Resuscitation (Revised 12/30/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-3: Newborn/Neonatal Assessment and Resuscitation — MDHHS Approved 12/30/22, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Infants less than 30 days old are considered neonates. This protocol is intended for assessment of newly born infants and/or the resuscitation of newly born infants less than 30 days old.

sections.title: History
points: Date and time of birth
Onset of symptoms
Prenatal history: prenatal care, substance abuse, multiple gestation, maternal illness
Birth history: maternal fever, meconium, prolapsed or nuchal cord, bleeding
Estimated gestational age (may be based on last menstrual period)

sections.title: Immediate Assessment — Respiratory (R of APGAR)
points: Assess rate and effort (strong, weak, or absent; regular or irregular)
Absent: if the baby does not breathe spontaneously, stimulate by gently rubbing its back or slapping the soles of its feet

Respiratory distress (grunting, nasal flaring, retractions, gasping, apnea OR no return of spontaneous breathing after stimulation): position airway (sniffing position) and clear airway as needed; if thick meconium or secretions present — suction mouth then nose; initiate ventilation with appropriately sized equipment and 21% oxygen (room air)

sections.title: Immediate Assessment — Heart Rate (P of APGAR)
points: Assess heart rate/pulse (fast, slow, or absent) — auscultation of chest is the preferred method

If heart rate > 100 bpm: monitor for central cyanosis, provide blow-by oxygen as needed; monitor for signs of respiratory distress — if apneic or significant distress, initiate BVM ventilation with room air at 40–60 breaths per minute

If heart rate < 100 bpm: initiate BVM ventilation with room air at 40–60 breaths per minute; primary indicator of improvement is increased heart rate; only use minimum necessary volume to achieve chest rise; if no improvement after 90 seconds, provide ventilations with supplemental oxygen (100%) until heart rate normalizes (100 or above)

If heart rate < 60 bpm: ensure effective ventilations with supplementary oxygen and adequate chest rise; if no improvements after 30 seconds, initiate chest compressions — two-thumb-encircling-hands technique is preferred; coordinate chest compressions with positive pressure ventilation (3:1 ratio — 90 compressions and 30 breaths per minute); per MCA selection, consider intubation per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol

sections.title: Immediate Assessment — Color, Grimace, Tone (A, G, A of APGAR)
points: COLOR/APPEARANCE (first A of APGAR): central cyanosis, peripheral cyanosis, pallor, or normal — administer blow-by oxygen for a few minutes until baby's core color is pink
GRIMACE (G of APGAR): reflex irritability to slap on sole of foot — score 0 (no response), 1 (crying/some motion), 2 (crying/vigorous)
MUSCLE TONE/ACTIVITY (second A of APGAR): poor or strong — score 0 (limp), 1 (some flexion), 2 (active/good motion)

sections.title: APGAR Scoring
points: APGAR score for witnessed deliveries should be noted at 1 minute and 5 minutes after delivery
A – Appearance (skin color): 0=bluish or pallor; 1=pink/ruddy body, blue extremities; 2=pink/ruddy entire body
P – Pulse (heart rate): 0=absent; 1=below 100; 2=over 100
G – Grimace (reflex irritability to foot slap): 0=no response; 1=crying/some motion; 2=crying/vigorous
A – Activity (muscle tone): 0=limp; 1=some flexion of extremities; 2=active/good motion
R – Respiration (respiratory effort): 0=absent; 1=slow and irregular; 2=normal/crying
Each parameter gets a score of 0 to 2

sections.title: Prevent Heat Loss
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 4-3: Newborn — Heat Loss Prevention
points: Maintain warm environment
Keep infant dry and covered with dry blankets
Keep infant's head covered with infant cap
Swaddle infant to mother skin to skin if infant is stable until transport
For patient transport: refer to Safe Transportation of Children in Ambulances-Treatment Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must indicate whether intubation is authorized during neonatal resuscitation for HR < 60 unresponsive to compressions and effective BVM ventilation

deterioration_flags: Heart rate < 100 bpm — BVM ventilation room air at 40–60 breaths/min; if no improvement after 90 seconds, switch to 100% O2
Heart rate < 60 bpm — chest compressions at 3:1 ratio with ventilation (90 compressions/30 breaths per minute); consider intubation per MCA selection; contact Medical Control
No return of spontaneous breathing after stimulation — position airway, clear secretions, BVM ventilation with room air
Thick meconium — suction mouth then nose before first breath; do NOT delay resuscitation for suctioning
scope_notes: Stimulation, positioning, blow-by oxygen, BVM ventilation: all levels
Intubation during neonatal resuscitation: per MCA selection — Specialist skill
APGAR scoring: all levels for witnessed deliveries
